label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Jane was born on the last day of February in 2000. Today is her 16th birthday. I need to find the date one week from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, so maybe I can work through them step by step. First, I need to figure out what today's date is. Since Jane was born on the last day of February 2000, that would be February 29, 2000, right? Because February 29 is a leap day, and it's only present in leap years. 2000 was a leap year because it's divisible by 400. So her birthday is on 2/29/2000. Now, today is her 16th birthday. But wait, leap years occur every 4 years. So 2000, 2004, 2008, 2012, 2016... So if she was born in 2000, her 16th birthday would be in 2016. Because 2000 + 16 = 2016. So today's date would be February 29, 2016. However, wait—2016 is a leap year because 2016 is divisible by 4. So yes, there is a February 29 in 2016. So today is 2/29/2016. Now, the question is asking for the date one week from today. Adding 7 days to February 29, 2016. Let's count the days. February has 29 days in 2016 (because it's a leap year). So adding 7 days: February 29 + 1 day = March 1 February 29 + 2 days = March 2 ... February 29 + 7 days = March 7, 2016. So one week from today would be March 7, 2016. Looking at the options, option A is 03/07/2016. But wait, let me check if there's any possible mistake here. Wait, another thought: sometimes when calculating dates, people might mistakenly assume that adding a week to February 29 would take you to March 7, but maybe in some cases, if the year doesn't have a leap day, it's different. But in this case, since we are starting on February 29, 2016 (a leap year), adding 7 days would indeed take us to March 7, 2016. So the correct date is 03/07/2016, which is option A. Let me check the options again to make sure. The options are: (A) 03/07/2016 (B) 02/23/2016 (C) 03/07/2070 (D) 03/06/2016 (E) 03/08/2016 So option A is the one that matches March 7, 2016. Therefore, the correct answer should be (A). Wait, but let me double-check the calculation again. If today is February 29, 2016, then adding 7 days: Day 1: March 1 (because February has 29 days in 2016, so March 1 is the next day) So 2/29 + 1 day = 3/1 Then adding 6 more days: 3/1 + 6 days = 3/7. Yes, that's correct. So one week from 2/29/2016 is 3/7/2016. So option A is correct. But let me check the other options just to be thorough. Option B is 02/23/2016. That would be 7 days before 3/1, which doesn't make sense. Option C is 2070, which is way off. Option D is March
